rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE UDELHOVEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Marcal Associates, Ltd.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Mono-kote fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McFall was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to sustain its burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar because defendant did not submit evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. By failing to submit verified factually devoid responses with its moving papers, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE ALCAINE et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Caterpillar Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Alcaine was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if plaintiffs had created a triable issue on that topic, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Alcaine¿s asbestos-related illness. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v.
